context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the output ordering and key naming convention used in the standard identity response handler
context_pointers.actual_meaning: the service must output 'client_id=<value>' followed by 'version=<value>' where both keys follow the specific pattern of 'prefix_identifier=value' used elsewhere in the codebase's response generation utilities

context_pointers.vague_reference: apply the same version format expected by the legacy authentication proxy
context_pointers.actual_meaning: the version string must adhere to the specific semantic versioning pattern (major.minor.patch) handled by the existing v1.x.x.proto service consumers

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_token_encode.json.description: Build a connection identity token from a client identity (a 128-bit GUID) and a four-part numeric mod version. A valid identity and version produce a single delimited token string of the form '<guid>%<version>'. Construction rejects an empty/zero identity and a missing version, each surfaced as a neutral argument_null error naming the offending field.

feature1_2_token_decode.json.description: Parse a connection identity token back into its identity and version fields. A well-formed '<guid>%<version>' token yields the recovered client identity and four-part version. Any malformed token — empty string, missing a part, an extra trailing delimiter, a leading delimiter, or a zero identity — is rejected as a neutral invalid_token error with no fields recovered.

feature2_1_modules_inspect.json.description: Summarize an installed module set into the count of modules and the game version. The game version is taken from the single module flagged as official; each module carries an id, an official flag, and a four-part numeric version. The reported game version is the official module's version formatted as four dot-separated numbers.

feature2_2_modules_compare.json.description: Compare two installed module sets for compatibility and for game-version agreement. Two sets are compatible only when every module (id, official flag, and full four-part version) in one set is also present in the other; compatibility is checked in both directions. Game versions agree only when the official module's version is identical across the two sets. Identical sets are mutually compatible and version-matching; sets differing in any version component are neither.

feature2_3_modules_serialize.json.description: Serialize each of two module sets to a portable payload and read it back. A payload deserialized back into a module set must be equal (mutually compatible) to the original set it came from. Two module sets that differ in any module version must produce distinct serialized payloads, so a payload unambiguously represents the set that produced it.

_constraints.input_condition: action is 'encode_token' and client_id is non-empty/non-zero and version is present
action is 'encode_token' and client_id is '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' and version is present
action is 'encode_token' and client_id is non-zero and version is missing
token is '<guid>%<version>' (valid format, non-zero guid, 4-part version)
token contains zero components (empty string, missing mid, missing right part)
token has leading, trailing, or double '%' delimiters
modules list populated and contains at least one official module
left manifests identical to right manifests (including versions)
left manifests differ from right manifests in any module version component

_constraints.output_rule: Output 'left_roundtrip_equal=true' (assuming valid serialization logic), 'right_roundtrip_equal=true', 'payloads_distinct=true' if versions differ
